story.hook: Chickens lock their view between steps.

story.description: A clip of forests, rainforest, canopy, moss, or forest ecosystems: lush green forest canopy view in summer
story.hook: Tree shows why the canopy shift matters.
story.lead: Look at that green blob clinging to the branch. That’s mistletoe, not moss—it’s a parasite stealing water from the tree. It grows berries that birds love, then poop them onto other branches. That’s why your local forest is full of these sneaky hitchhikers. Did you ever spot one and assume it was harmless?
story.script: Look at that green blob clinging to the branch. That’s mistletoe, not moss—it’s a parasite stealing water from the tree. story.description: A clip of farm animals: duck preening on rock in calm lake waters
story.hook: Ducks show why the waterproof feathers matters.
story.lead: Ducks aren’t just primping. See those oil glands near their tail. They rub it on every feather. That’s why water beads up like this—it’s a built-in raincoat. And those little flips. They’re zipping oil down each strand. Feathers stay dry because that oil repels water faster than you think.

story.description: A clip of farm animals: two white chickens walking on pavement
story.hook: Chickens lock their view between steps
story.lead: City chickens look lost, but here's the part nobody tells you: that's exactly what they're doing. These two are checking pavement like sidewalks, scanning for scraps. Chickens don't need roads, that's why they just stroll any flat surface. Ever seen one jog like this? Think about it next time you spot a chicken in town.
story.script: City chickens look lost, but here's the part nobody tells you: that's exactly what they're doing. These two are checking pavement like sidewalks, scanning for scraps. story.hook: Chickens lock their view between steps.
story.lead: Chickens lock their view between steps. Watch the eyes stay level while the body steps forward, because chickens stabilize their view in tiny pauses. That helps them judge distance and spot danger without the world blurring. Once you see the pause, the walk feels different. Did you notice it?

story.hook: Ducks keep water sliding off their feathers

story.description: A clip of dogs / puppies: a dog playing with water
story.hook: Dogs can follow a human point fast
story.lead: Dogs aren't just splashing around. Watch how they use their paws like little mops. Every slap traps water, then they drink it right off. story.description: A clip of farm animals: a cow and a calf are walking in the desert
story.hook: Cows can remember who belongs in the herd
story.lead: Desert cows don't need water every day. Watch this pair move slow but their hooves keep cool in sand that would burn your feet. That's why they trek miles to water every three days instead of daily. How do you think they'd fare in your backyard.
